title: Electron self-energy
summary: The self-energy captures exchange and correlation corrections beyond a mean-field reference and shifts quasiparticle energies.
assumptions: A nonlocal, energy-dependent self-energy is the right correction object for the targeted excitation problem.

regime.domain: ab initio many-body perturbation theory for charged excitations
boundary_conditions: reference orbitals remain a useful starting basis

applies_to: GW and QSGW updates of charged excitation energies
out_of_scope: pure total-energy functionals without an excitation interpretation

promotion.rationale: Manual L2 seed for reusable GW/QSGW excitation memory.

payload.definition: Exchange-correlation operator Sigma(omega) entering Dyson's equation.
payload.operational_role: Provides the quasiparticle correction relative to the reference Hamiltonian.
